$schema_note: Over-regularisation MCQ distractor bank. Each record: the correct irregular form and the plausible over-regularised wrong form(s) a learner produces by applying a regular rule. Intended primarily as INDEX-SCORED MCQ distractors (see ingestion note): many distractors are real words (see 'collisions'), so they are UNSAFE as substring must_not_include guards.

proposed_misconception_ids.reason: family 'regularisation' claims irregular plurals as cross-kind but has no leaf; needed for braccia/uova/dita/uomini/paia and the sovrabbondanti
family 'regularisation' claims irregular comparatives as cross-kind but has no leaf; needed for migliore/peggiore/maggiore/minore/meglio/peggio
no leaf for regularising an invariable noun (film->filmi, città->cittadi, foto->foti)

records.rule_misapplied: conjugated andare as a regular -are verb on the stem 'and-' instead of the suppletive 'vad-'
applied the regular -are 3pl ending to the suppletive stem
conjugated fare regularly on the reduced stem 'fa-/fac-' instead of the irregular 'faccio'
built the 2pl on the full Latin stem 'fac-' (facete) instead of the contracted 'fate'
applied a regular 3pl ending to 'fac-' instead of the irregular 'fanno'
treated stare as a fully regular -are verb (sta+o)
regular -are 3pl on 'sta-' instead of irregular 'stanno'
conjugated sapere regularly (sap+o) instead of the irregular monosyllable 'so'
regular -ere 3pl on 'sap-' instead of irregular 'sanno'

collisions.valid_as: adjective 'sano' (healthy)

records.rule_misapplied: used the regular masc plural 'ossi' where the body-bones sense takes 'ossa'

collisions.valid_as: 'ossi' IS correct for detached/animal bones (e.g. dog bones, fruit stones)

collisions.valid_as: 'muri' IS correct for walls generally (individual walls)

collisions.valid_as: 'ginocchi' is an accepted alternative plural

records.rule_misapplied: used the regular masc plural 'lenzuoli' where the set-of-sheets sense takes 'lenzuola'

collisions.valid_as: 'lenzuoli' IS correct for individual sheets

collisions.valid_as: 'cigli' IS correct for edges/verges (e.g. of a road)

collisions.valid_as: 'labbri' IS correct for rims/edges (e.g. of a wound or vessel)

records.rule_misapplied: used the regular masc plural 'membri' where the body-limbs sense takes 'membra'

collisions.valid_as: 'membri' IS correct for members of a group

records.rule_misapplied: restored a Latin-style -di plural (cittadi) on an invariable accented noun that stays 'città'
applied a regular -i plural (filmi) to an invariable loanword that stays 'film'
applied a regular plural (foti) to an invariable clipped noun (foto<fotografia) that stays 'foto'
applied a regular -i plural (caffei) to an invariable accented noun that stays 'caffè'
applied a regular plural (rei) to an invariable monosyllable that stays 're'
applied a regular plural (crise) to an invariable -i noun that stays 'crisi'
applied a plural ending to an invariable accented noun that stays 'università'
kept the hard-c spelling (amichi); stressed-antepenult -co nouns soften to -ci (amici)
kept the hard-c spelling (medichi) instead of the soft 'medici'
dropped the h that keeps the g hard (agi) instead of 'aghi'

collisions.valid_as: 'agi' is a real word: plural of 'agio' (ease/comfort)

records.rule_misapplied: dropped the h that keeps the c hard (fuoci) instead of 'fuochi'
dropped the h that keeps the c hard (amice) instead of 'amiche'
dropped the h that keeps the c hard (barce) instead of 'barche'
dropped the i that is kept after a vowel+cia (camice) instead of 'camicie'

collisions.valid_as: 'camice' is a real word (a medical/lab coat)

records.rule_misapplied: kept the i after a consonant+cia (arancie) instead of the required 'arance'
kept the i after a consonant+gia (pioggie) instead of the required 'piogge'
doubled the i on an unstressed -io noun (figlii) instead of the single-i 'figli'
used a single i on a stressed -io noun (zi) instead of the double-i 'zii'
pluralised the first element (capilavori) instead of only the head noun 'capolavori'
formed the comparative with regular 'più + buono' where the quality sense needs suppletive 'migliore', or double-marked 'più migliore'

collisions.valid_as: 'più buono' IS acceptable in the concrete sense (tastier / kinder); wrong only where abstract 'better in quality' needs migliore

records.rule_misapplied: regular 'più + cattivo' where the quality sense needs 'peggiore', or double-marked 'più peggiore'

collisions.valid_as: 'più cattivo' IS acceptable in the concrete/moral sense (naughtier); wrong where 'worse in quality' needs peggiore

records.rule_misapplied: regular 'più + grande' where the abstract sense needs 'maggiore', or double-marked 'più maggiore'

collisions.valid_as: 'più grande' IS correct for physically bigger; 'maggiore' is for greater/elder/more important

records.rule_misapplied: regular 'più + piccolo' where the abstract sense needs 'minore', or double-marked 'più minore'

collisions.valid_as: 'più piccolo' IS correct for physically smaller; 'minore' is for lesser/younger/minor

records.rule_misapplied: formed the comparative of the adverb 'bene' with 'più' (più bene) instead of the suppletive 'meglio'
formed the comparative of the adverb 'male' with 'più' (più male) instead of the suppletive 'peggio'
stacked 'più' on 'molto' (più molto) instead of the suppletive comparative 'di più'
formed the comparative of 'poco' with 'più' instead of the suppletive 'di meno'/'meno'
used the adjective superlative 'buonissimo' where the adverb 'bene' takes 'benissimo'

collisions.valid_as: 'buonissimo' IS the valid superlative of the ADJECTIVE buono; wrong only as the adverb superlative

records.rule_misapplied: used the adjective superlative 'cattivissimo' where the adverb 'male' takes 'malissimo'

collisions.valid_as: 'cattivissimo' IS the valid superlative of the ADJECTIVE cattivo; wrong only as the adverb superlative

records.rule_misapplied: over-marked the already-superlative-strength irregular; note 'buonissimo' is an accepted regular variant of 'ottimo'

collisions.valid_as: 'buonissimo' is ACCEPTED alongside 'ottimo' - not a reliable distractor

records.rule_misapplied: over-applied the default 'il' before s+consonant/z/gn/ps/x/y, which require 'lo' (lo studente, lo zaino)
over-applied the default 'i' before vowels/s+consonant/z, which require 'gli' (gli amici, gli studenti)
failed to elide before a vowel, using il/lo instead of l' (l'amico)
over-applied the marked 'lo' before a plain consonant, which takes 'il' (il libro)
over-applied 'gli' before a plain consonant, which takes 'i' (i libri)
over-applied the default 'un' before s+consonant/z/gn/ps, which require 'uno' (uno studente, uno zaino)
over-applied the marked 'uno' where 'un' is required (un libro, un amico); un takes no apostrophe before masc vowels
used the full form 'bello' before a plain consonant, which takes the shortened 'bel' (bel ragazzo)
shortened to 'bel' before s+consonant/z, which keeps 'bello' (bello zaino)
used 'bei'/'belli' before vowels/s+consonant, which require 'begli' (begli amici, begli studenti)
used the non-prenominal 'belli' (or 'begli') before a plain consonant, which takes 'bei' (bei libri)
used the full 'quello' before a plain consonant, which takes 'quel' (quel libro)
shortened to 'quel' before s+consonant/z, which keeps 'quello' (quello studente, quello zaino)
used 'quei'/'quelli' before vowels/s+consonant, which require 'quegli' (quegli amici, quegli studenti)
used the pronoun 'quelli' (or 'quegli') before a plain consonant, which takes 'quei' (quei libri)
used the full 'buono' where prenominal masc sg shortens to 'buon' (buon ragazzo, buon amico)
shortened to 'buon' before s+consonant/z, which keeps 'buono' (buono studente)
kept the full 'grande' where it optionally shortens to 'gran' before a consonant (un gran successo); note 'grande' is also accepted here

collisions.valid_as: 'grande' is also acceptable prenominally; the shortening to 'gran' is optional

records.rule_misapplied: used the full 'santo' before a masc name starting with a consonant, which takes 'san' (san Marco)
failed to elide before a vowel-initial name, which takes sant' (sant'Antonio)
shortened to 'san' before an s+consonant name, which keeps 'santo' (santo Stefano)
